title: The Minimal Blog Demo
author: Anthony
content:
Welcome to the Minimal Blog Demo. This app is designed to show a clean reading experience with modern spacing, calm typography, and distraction-free presentation.

For the demo, posts are rendered dynamically from a JSON data source. That means the feed and details are not hardcoded in the UI. Every card in the feed is generated by mapping through data and applying reusable components.

The goal is simple: make content feel premium without visual noise. Strong hierarchy, readable body text, and smooth scrolling are all intentional choices to keep attention on the story.

From here, you can evolve this into Markdown content, CMS integration, or a Supabase table-backed publishing flow. But this MVP already proves the complete experience end-to-end.

title: Designing for Readability on Mobile
author: Anthony
content:
Reading on a phone is different from reading on a desktop. The viewport is tighter, attention spans are shorter, and dense layout quickly feels overwhelming.

A strong mobile reading layout uses generous horizontal padding, comfortable line height, and a clear progression from title to metadata to body. If every element competes for attention, users lose the narrative thread.

In this app, cards are intentionally quiet: rounded corners, subtle borders, and enough white space to breathe. On detail pages, content sits in a ScrollView with typographic rhythm that feels natural over long passages.

Minimal design is not about removing everything. It is about removing everything that does not support the message.

title: Shipping Reliable Demos with Auth Fallback
author: Anthony
content:
Live demos fail for avoidable reasons: unstable Wi-Fi, OAuth redirect mistakes, environment variables, and tunnel setup issues. A polished demo plan includes fallback paths that keep the presentation moving.

For authentication, Google OAuth through Supabase is ideal for real-world credibility. But when network conditions are unpredictable, a guest mode can preserve the user flow and still demonstrate core product value.

The best demo architecture is resilient by default. Keep setup instructions clear, document redirect URLs carefully, and include troubleshooting notes for LAN versus tunnel behavior in Expo.

When reliability improves, confidence improves. And confidence is what makes your product look production-ready.
